intro:
There is no information on Trigonella besseriana. However, I can provide information on Trigonella foenum-graecum, commonly known as fenugreek, which is a member of the Fabaceae family. Trigonella foenum-graecum is an annual herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its small yellow flowers and trifoliate leaves.

Trigonella foenum-graecum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve digestion, and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The bioactive constituents of fenugreek include saponins, flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with fenugreek include teas, capsules, and tinctures, often used to support metabolic health and alleviate symptoms of menopause.
